prompt: The switch is intended to print only the matching season, but it prints extra output. What is the bug?
choices: switch cannot use multiple case labels on one line
Missing break after each season block causes fall-through — month=6 prints Summer and Fall
case 6 is invalid because 6 is not a compile-time constant
The default case is required for switch on int
explanation: Without break statements, execution falls through from the matching case to every subsequent case. month=6 matches case 6, prints "Summer", then falls through to case 9 and prints "Fall". Fix: add break; (or return;) at the end of each season block.
